topic: Identify which NEW features, optimizations, automations, and integrations a Rust component could ADD or IMPROVE for the sk-design styles database, explicitly not a like-for-like rewrite.

noveltyJustification: This first iteration converted the broad ANN question into scale-specific gates, distinguished in-database exact search from ANN, and identified repository ABI precedent plus a potentially material Rust-only filtering capability.
focus: Pressure-test ANN and in-database vector-search options against retrieval.mjs at 1,290, 10x, and 100x scale.
answeredQuestions: Which vector-search architecture, if any, unlocks meaningful capability at 1,290, 10x, and 100x bundles?

ruledOut.approach: Custom Rust HNSW at current scale
ruledOut.reason: Maintained native bindings expose HNSW while no measured styles workload clears the ownership gate.
The inspected public contract establishes in-database KNN but not a shipped ANN index.
No browser requirement makes its portability material.
No current scale, memory, or rebuild requirement offsets training complexity.

graphEvents.label: sqlite-vec is exact integration, not ANN
Rust value is filter-aware ANN, not HNSW alone

noveltyJustification: This iteration converted the qualitative scale gate into measurable latency and utilization thresholds and found that broad 100x requests can exceed SQLite's default host-parameter ceiling before ANN performance is relevant.
focus: Define measured p50/p95 latency, vector dimension, eligible-row distribution, and query-rate triggers for the 10x and 100x migration gates.

graphEvents.label: Use staged p50 p95 and utilization gates

noveltyJustification: This iteration added the first host-bound full-corpus replay measurements, proved that no active embedding profile exists, and separated measured legacy service throughput from the still-missing production arrival and vector-stage evidence.
focus: Measure production or replayed eligibility percentiles, active embedding dimensions, stage p50/p95 values, and query-rate distribution on a declared reference host.

noveltyJustification: This iteration located the exact Rust residency boundary, identified local batched inference as the only clear new capability, and converted hashing, watching, and caching ideas into measured conditional gates or ruled-out ownership transfers.
focus: Which indexing and embedding automations become materially better with Rust?
answeredQuestions: Which indexing and embedding automations become materially better with Rust?

ruledOut.reason: JavaScript and SQLite already own lifecycle and atomic publication; only parallel precomputation is conditionally material.

ruledOut.approach: Rust filesystem watcher as correctness authority
ruledOut.reason: Filesystem events can be lost or reshaped and must remain hints for canonical rescans.
The existing retrieval-hash and profile cache already provides semantic content-addressed reuse.
The single transaction is a correctness boundary rather than incidental serialization.

noveltyJustification: This iteration removed system-code-graph from the assumed shared workload, found an existing resident batch-inference plane in Spec Kit Memory, and narrowed any viable cross-system Rust work to a two-consumer inference ABI with explicit evidence gates.
focus: Is a shared Rust inference/search core worth cross-system contract and release coordination?
answeredQuestions: Is a shared Rust inference/search core worth cross-system contract and release coordination?

ruledOut.reason: system-code-graph is deliberately structural, embedding-free, skill-local, and CI-isolated.
Styles and Spec Memory have incompatible policy, persistence, publication, and fallback contracts.

ruledOut.approach: Resident Rust worker as novelty by itself
ruledOut.reason: Spec Memory already ships a resident batched local-model service.
Cache identity and atomic publication are subsystem-local correctness boundaries.

graphEvents.label: Shared Rust inference and search core
Code graph has no vector workload
Spec Memory already has resident batched inference
Only inference ABI is plausibly shared
No shared Rust search core today
Code graph isolation and ownership contract

noveltyJustification: This iteration located an already-rich DOM geometry plane, exposed the missing screenshot artifact/publication contract, separated perceptual dedupe from semantic retrieval, and narrowed Rust's only plausible visual role to measured multimodal model execution.
focus: Pressure-test screenshot palette and spacing extraction, perceptual hashing, layout fingerprints, and multimodal embeddings; classify which need Rust versus existing JS/native tools.
answeredQuestions: Which new visual/style-analysis features are practical?
Which visual-analysis features need Rust?
Can visual features reuse the resident model-service shape?

ruledOut.approach: Screenshot-first spacing extraction for live pages
ruledOut.reason: The existing crawler already captures DOM rectangles and computed spacing exactly.
Native-backed Node image tooling provides the initial decode and statistics baseline.

ruledOut.approach: Perceptual hash as semantic style rank
ruledOut.reason: Perceptual distance measures near-duplicate visual content rather than design-language similarity.

ruledOut.approach: Reuse the text embedding ABI unchanged for images
ruledOut.reason: Image modality requires distinct preprocessing, profile identity, payload, and cache semantics.

ruledOut.approach: Monolithic Rust visual-analysis worker before measurement
ruledOut.reason: It couples independent features and release boundaries without proven compute residency.

graphEvents.label: Perceptual hash serves dedupe and drift
Multimodal retrieval needs a new ABI
Rust is conditional for model execution only
Design crawler screenshots and element styles

noveltyJustification: This iteration separated offline/privacy value from implementation language, established the installed ONNX stack as the mandatory local multimodal oracle, distinguished ort from Candle by parity and packaging risk, and converted Rust adoption into worker-isolation and artifact-contract gates at 10x/100x scale.
focus: Assess on-device local text and image embedding models through Rust runtimes such as ONNX Runtime or Candle: capability, model residency, packaging, privacy/offline value, JS/native-addon alternatives, and current/10x/100x gates.
answeredQuestions: Does local text/image inference require Rust?
Which Rust runtime is the default candidate?
Should a resident model use an addon or sidecar?
What clears the current, 10x, and 100x runtime gates?

ruledOut.approach: Rust as privacy or offline enabler
ruledOut.reason: Locality follows model provisioning and network policy, not host language.

ruledOut.approach: Rust ort rewrite for inference speed
ruledOut.reason: Node and Rust wrappers execute the same native ONNX Runtime kernels.

ruledOut.approach: In-process native addon as default model host
ruledOut.reason: It adds no kernel residency and loses sidecar crash and RSS isolation.

ruledOut.approach: Bundle model weights in npm or native binary
ruledOut.reason: Weights are large, licensed, profile-specific artifacts with an independent lifecycle.
Each model architecture, preprocessing path, backend, and numerical contract requires separate qualification.

graphEvents.label: Rust gate for local text and image models
Existing Node stack supports offline multimodal inference
Privacy and offline value are runtime-neutral
Rust ort and Node share native kernels
Models need an opt-in verified store
Rust worker is conditional at 10x and 100x

noveltyJustification: This iteration found the cross-generation parsed-projection cache as the real missing capability, measured current hashing cost, isolated the safe bounded pre-transaction worker seam, and proved that watcher correctness and external-artifact publication are separate contracts.
focus: Assess content-addressed caches, incremental hashing, parallel streaming corpus indexing, and a Rust file watcher for automatic reindex.
answeredQuestions: Which indexing automations materially benefit from Rust today?
Is a new content-addressed cache useful?
Does a Rust watcher improve correctness?
Where must atomicity remain?
What is the scale gate?

ruledOut.approach: Replace existing content identities with a Rust cache
ruledOut.reason: Aggregate, retrieval, generation, and embedding content identities already exist; only cross-generation parsed-projection reuse is missing.
Current hashing is not material at present scale and the change would break persisted identity and oracle parity.
The single transaction is the correctness boundary; bounded parallelism belongs in verification and parsing.
Node, Chokidar, and Rust notify all consume fallible platform events, so authoritative reconciliation remains required.

ruledOut.approach: Standalone Rust watcher at current scale
ruledOut.reason: Chokidar provides normalized atomic and chunked-write handling without a new binary lifecycle.

noveltyJustification: This iteration proved that candidate caps decouple RRF cost from corpus growth, identified the current test oracle's exact parity gaps, and made adapter selection derivative of an independently material resident or unbounded workload.
focus: Pressure-test deterministic ranking kernels, reusable pure-core boundaries, and TS-oracle parity across napi-rs/WASM/sidecar choices.

graphEvents.label: Styles retrieval candidate and fusion contract

noveltyJustification: This final iteration converted nine evidence rounds into one ranked gate matrix, made the multi-artifact manifest and oracle the unconditional first phase, and separated worthwhile capability work from conditional Rust residency.
focus: Adjudicate and rank the complete opportunity set, identify not-worth-Rust calls, and define phased adoption preserving TypeScript ownership and pinned-oracle parity.
answeredQuestions: Which vector-search architecture unlocks capability at 1x, 10x, and 100x?
Which indexing and embedding automations materially benefit from Rust?
Which visual and multimodal features are practical?
Is a shared Rust search core worthwhile?
Which opportunities clear residency, materiality, and scale gates?

ruledOut.reason: Ranking, hashing, caching, watching, SQLite publication, and full indexing either already run natively or add no new capability.
Code graph has no vector workload and the two retrieval systems have incompatible policy and publication contracts.
Maintained exact and HNSW candidates plus bounded SQL eligibility must be exhausted first.

ruledOut.approach: Query-visible external artifacts outside the generation manifest
ruledOut.reason: They cannot be atomically bound to the authoritative SQLite generation.

graphEvents.label: Manifest and TS oracle come first
No Rust component clears all current gates

reason: detached lineage scope permits writes only inside fanout lineage artifact directory
